key_points: The lesson is written by Robert W. Peterson
Richard Ebright is the central character of the lesson
Ebright showed curiosity from a young age
He was interested in collecting butterflies
His mother played a major role in encouraging his learning
The lesson explains how a scientist is developed through effort
Failure helped Ebright understand real scientific learning
Experimentation is the core of true science
Curiosity and perseverance are key qualities of a scientist
important_characters: Ebright’s mother – his guide and supporter
Dr Frederick A. Urquhart – scientific mentor
important_events: Reading the book ‘The Travels of Monarch X’
message_of_the_lesson: A scientist is made, not born
Hard work and perseverance bring success
exam_tips: Remember the role of Ebright’s mother
Focus on the science fair incident
Highlight curiosity and experimentation in answers
Explain the title clearly in exams
